title: We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale

{{Quote|When Jabba frowns... somebody dies.|[[Doikk Na'ts/Legends|Doikk Na'ts]]|<ref name="We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale">{{StoryCite|story=We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref>}}
"'''We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale'''" is a short story by [[Kathy Tyers]] from ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]'', which was published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[August]] [[1995]], about [[Figrin D'an and the Modal Nodes/Legends|Figrin D'an and the Modal Nodes]]. It takes place in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. This is the first of two stories from the collection to be made into a [[We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale (audio drama)|full-cast audio drama]].

==Plot summary==
[[Figrin D'an/Legends|Figrin D'an]] and the [[Figrin D'an and the Modal Nodes|Modal Nodes]] are members of the [[Intergalactic Federation of Musicians]] specializing in a style of [[Music/Legends|music]] known as [[Jizz/Legends|jizz]]. While working on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], they enter into an exclusive contract to work solely for the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] known as [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]. Figrin, however, wants to get out of his contract.

One of Jabba's rivals, a [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] named [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]], sends an [[E522 assassin droid/Legends|E522 assassin droid]] to [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] to recruit Figrin D'an's services. Valarian offers them 3,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to perform at her [[Marriage/Legends|wedding]] to the Whiphid hunter, [[D'Wopp]]. The allure of Valarian's [[Sabacc/Legends|Sabacc]] tables notwithstanding, Figrin feels that this would present an excellent opportunity to earn enough money to book passage off of Tatooine and get out from under the thumb of Jabba the Hutt.

The band sneaks off to [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] and sets their equipment up at the [[Star Chamber Café]] inside of Valarian's popular nightspot, the [[Lucky Despot|''Lucky Despot'' Hotel & Casino]]. They began playing at the wedding reception, but during the stage breaks, Figrin begins spending the band's hard-earned credits at the Sabacc tables.

Jabba learns of this breach of contract and sends several of his henchmen down to the ''Lucky Despot'' to disrupt the festivities. This also allows Jabba the opportunity to [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] his rival's enterprises. As luck would have it, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] likewise raid the [[Casino/Legends|casino]]. The band quickly gathers their equipment and scurries outside.

The [[Dorenian Beshniquel/Legends|Dorenian Beshniquel]] player known as [[Doikk Na'ts/Legends|Doikk Na'ts]] meets with one of the party guests, an untidy [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Wuher/Legends|Wuher]]. Wuher invites the band to play at his establishment down the street – [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]]. The Modal Nodes accept the offer, but with the caveat that they will be leaving the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] planet at the first given opportunity.

==Continuity==
This story was adapted by [[Charles Potter]] as a full-cast [[We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale (audio drama)|audio dramatization]] and released on [[Wikipedia: Compact disc|CD]] and [[Wikipedia: Cassette tape|cassette]] by [[Bantam Doubleday Dell Audio Publishing|Bantam Doubleday]] in [[1995]]. [[Figrin D'an/Legends|Figrin D'an]] was originally spelled “Figrin Da'n” in ''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'' ([[1989]]), the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] supplement that first gave the [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Music/Legends|musician]] his name.<ref name="TERC" />
